title: Advanced Portuguese
catalogDescription: Emphasizes oral skills while continuing to refine reading comprehension and written expression. Aims to develop abilities to successfully deal with comprehension and production of oral texts of an academic and professional nature. Not a conversation course. Prerequisite: PORT 203.

title: Brazilian, Portuguese and African Cultures (5-12, max. 15) A&H/SSc
catalogDescription: Explores cultures of Brazil, Portugal, and Portuguese-speaking countries in Africa within the framework of cultural studies theory. Follows an interdisciplinary approach, drawing from readings, films, history, literature, arts, and anthropology, among others. Focuses on selected cultural aspects and countries. Taught in English. Offered: jointly with JSIS A 365; S.

title: Port-Language Track for Mapping Luso-Brazilian Cultures
catalogDescription: Language track option in Portuguese for intermediate or higher level students, in conjunction with PORT 365/JSIS A 365. Must be registered in PORT 365/JSIS A 365, to take this class. Explores readings and films/documentaries in the cultures of Brazil and the Portuguese-speaking world. Prerequisite: PORT 201 or permission of instructor. Offered: Sp.
prerequisiteNotes: Official UW prerequisite text: PORT 201 or permission of instructor.
